mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during low-rate periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength across durable and non-durable sectors, driving higher corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which immediately boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches or financial stress periods, producing negative 5-day returns despite rising industrial output, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in stress regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, concurrent monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls amplify the transmission of monetary accommodation to the real economy, accelerating earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when inflation expectations rise above 2.5%, or the combined signal yields a negative predictive coefficient (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation prints above the threshold due to supply-side shocks or commodity supercycles, yielding positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark over 3-year rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales below 0.2 percent signals weakening consumer demand and impending earnings revisions, which immediately pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales dips fail to predict negative returns when accompanied by inventory drawdowns, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
